mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations and stimulates corporate borrowing, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series or yields negative returns in more than 50% of co-occurring expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-recession expansion phase reflects accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle expansions or Rank IC remains negative when filtered for recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate falling below 1.5 percent indicates stagnating aggregate demand and weak corporate earnings momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in over 65% of low-growth quarters or the threshold fails to separate return distributions at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate creates a real rate tightening environment that compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power or positive returns dominate when inflation rises alongside rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls during an economic expansion phase confirm robust labor market health and rising household income, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth ceases to predict positive returns when unemployment exceeds 6 percent or the signal yields negative alpha in late-cycle filters.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 55% of double-dip contraction months or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
